description: Moving or operating at a low speed; not hurried. 'Sheru' names the quality of unhurried pace—movement that takes its time, action that refuses to rush.
sound_symbolism: The word moves gently. 'She' /ʃe/ begins with soft breath, haste released. Then 'ru' rolls slowly, deliberation embodied.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'sheru telo' (slow work). Central to Phi's philosophy—slowness enables mindfulness, rushing prevents presence.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Slow action enables awareness—rushing through life misses the living of it.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional crafts moved slowly—quality emerged from patience, not speed.
